title: Help protect my device with Windows Security
license_note: Screenshots from support.microsoft.com. Confirm redistribution with your legal team before use in a shipped product.

sections.title: Schedule a scan in Windows Security

steps.text: In the search box on the taskbar, type Task Scheduler and then select Task Scheduler in the list of results.
In the left pane of Task Scheduler, expand Task Scheduler Library > Microsoft > Windows and then scroll down and double-click the Windows Defender folder.
In the top-center pane, double-click Windows Defender Scheduled Scan .
Select the Triggers tab, and then select New .
Set your time and frequency, and then select OK .
steps.caption: Scheduling a Windows Security scan in Task Scheduler

steps.text: Select the Start button, then select Settings > Update & Security > Windows Security > Virus & threat protection .
Do one of the following: In the current version of Windows 10: Under Virus & threat protection settings , select Manage settings , and then switch the Real-time protection setting to On or Off .
In previous versions of Windows 10: Select Virus & threat protection settings , and then switch the Real-time protection setting to On or Off .
